brownfield_phase_10b.description: Brownfield Discovery Phase 10b (story creation) — runtime override for agent authority conflict
brownfield_phase_10b.conflict: Three different owners across three artifacts: workflow says @pm, task says @po, agent-authority says @sm exclusively owns *draft/*create-story
brownfield_phase_10b.override: Delegate to @sm for story creation, NOT @pm. The agent-authority.md rule is authoritative (Constitutional).
brownfield_phase_10b.enforcement: When executing brownfield-discovery.yaml Phase 10b step 'story_creation', route to @sm *draft instead of @pm *create-story.

spec_quality_checklist_missing.description: spec-critique.md references spec-quality-checklist.md for selfCritique.checklistRef, but the file does not exist anywhere in .aiox-core/
spec_quality_checklist_missing.impact: Self-critique step in spec-critique.md cannot load its validation checklist
spec_quality_checklist_missing.fallback: Use story-lifecycle.md Phase 4 QA checks (7 quality checks) as substitute criteria: (1) code review patterns, (2) unit tests, (3) acceptance criteria, (4) no regressions, (5) performance, (6) security, (7) documentation.
spec_quality_checklist_missing.remediation: Create .aiox-core/development/checklists/spec-quality-checklist.md derived from spec-critique.md dimension checks (accuracy, completeness, consistency, feasibility, alignment) or resolve via framework update.

spec_tmpl_path.description: spec-write-spec.md references template path development/templates/spec-tmpl.md but the file lives at product/templates/spec-tmpl.md
spec_tmpl_path.runtime_workaround: When executing spec-write-spec, load template from product/templates/spec-tmpl.md (not development/templates/).

development_cycle.description: development-cycle.yaml references task names that do not match actual task filenames in development/tasks/

mapping_table.develop: NO MATCHING FILE (use dev-develop-story.md or build-component.md)
mapping_table.self-heal: NO MATCHING FILE (functionality embedded in coderabbit-integration.md rules)
mapping_table.quality-review: NO MATCHING FILE (use qa-gate.md or agent-quality-gate.md)
mapping_table.push-and-pr: NO MATCHING FILE (use add-mcp.md or ci-cd-configuration.md, but @devops exclusive)

development_cycle.mismatch_count: 5 of 6 task names do not match actual filenames
development_cycle.runtime_workaround: Resolve as follows: validate-story-draft -> validate-next-story.md; develop -> dev-develop-story.md; self-heal -> apply coderabbit-integration.md rules directly; quality-review -> qa-gate.md; push-and-pr -> delegate to @devops.
development_cycle.recommendation: Update development-cycle.yaml task references to match actual filenames, or rename task files to match workflow names.

spec_pipeline_simple_skip.description: spec-pipeline.yaml assess step has skip_if condition that can skip complexity.json generation, but research step's skip_if condition depends on complexity.json existing
spec_pipeline_simple_skip.gap: If assess step is skipped (source === 'simple' or overrideComplexity === 'SIMPLE'), complexity.json is never created. Research step later checks skip_if: 'complexity.result === SIMPLE' but complexity.json is absent, causing a reference error.
spec_pipeline_simple_skip.runtime_guard: Before evaluating research step's skip_if condition, verify complexity.json exists. If absent (assess was skipped), treat complexity as SIMPLE and skip research automatically.
spec_pipeline_simple_skip.scenario_1: SIMPLE pipeline path (gather -> spec -> critique): research step is not in the steps list, so no issue. Guard is still recommended for edge cases.
spec_pipeline_simple_skip.scenario_2: STANDARD pipeline with SIMPLE override: assess skipped, complexity.json absent, research runs but skip_if references nonexistent field. GUARD REQUIRED.
spec_pipeline_simple_skip.remediation: Update spec-pipeline.yaml research step to: (1) check if complexity.json exists first, (2) if absent default to skip research phase, OR add guard logic to assess step to always generate a minimal complexity.json when skipped.

state_file_mismatch.description: epic-orchestration.yaml (line 180-182) declares state persistence at .aiox/workflow-state/${epicId}-pipeline.json (JSON format), but execute-epic-plan.md writes to .aiox/epic-{epicId}-state.yaml (YAML format)
state_file_mismatch.impact: State file written by execute-epic-plan.md cannot be found at the path expected by epic-orchestration.yaml template. Resume across sessions via execute-epic-plan.md action=continue works (uses .aiox/epic-{epicId}-state.yaml path directly), but the orchestration template cannot read the state.
state_file_mismatch.resolution: Runtime canonical location is .aiox/epic-{epicId}-state.yaml (YAML format), matching execute-epic-plan.md implementation. .aiox/workflow-state/ directory created with README documenting the mismatch.

execution_yaml_missing.description: 8 epics exist in docs/stories/epics/, none have EPIC-{ID}-EXECUTION.yaml execution plans required by epic-orchestration
execution_yaml_missing.impact: No epic can currently be executed via the epic-orchestration workflow. This is expected — epic-orchestration has not been used in this project yet.
execution_yaml_missing.expected_first_epic_note: DATA-FOUNDATION epic will be the first to use epic-orchestration properly with a full EXECUTION.yaml plan. The remaining 7 epics (EPIC-COVERAGE-MAX-200KM, EPIC-MASTER-B2G-READINESS, epic-001-100-cobertura, epic-b2g-max-evolution, epic-coverage-100pct, epic-master-b2g, epic-td-001-resolution, epic-td-002-code-quality, epic-td-003-reversa-remediation, epic-feat-001-crawlers-coverage) do not require execution plans until they are prioritized for wave-based execution.

po_epic_context_missing.description: execute-epic-plan.md dependencies list (line 241) references po-epic-context.md, but this markdown documentation file does not exist. The JS implementation exists at .aiox-core/core/orchestration/epic-context-accumulator.js
po_epic_context_missing.impact: PO cannot reference po-epic-context.md for context accumulation during epic validation. The functionality exists but is undocumented in markdown form.

module_api.main_method_params: storyN (0-based index of current story), options.filesToModify (string[]), options.executor (string)

po_epic_context_missing.runtime_workaround: PO should use EpicContextAccumulator JS module directly until po-epic-context.md documentation is created. Import via: require('.aiox-core/core/orchestration/epic-context-accumulator').createEpicContextAccumulator(sessionState).buildAccumulatedContext(epicId, storyN, { filesToModify, executor })
po_epic_context_missing.remediation: Create docs/stories/epics/po-epic-context.md documenting the module API for PO use during epic validation

gate_agent_hardcoded.description: execute-epic-plan.md hardcodes subagent_type: 'aiox-architect' for wave gate (line 622) and final gate (line 726), while epic-orchestration.yaml uses ${wave.gate_agent} (dynamic, line 138)
gate_agent_hardcoded.impact: Wave gate agent cannot be customized per wave via the execution plan. All gates default to aiox-architect regardless of wave risk profile.
gate_agent_hardcoded.runtime_override_rule: Wave gate agent is read from execution plan's waves[].gate_agent field. If present, that value overrides the hardcoded 'aiox-architect'. Default behavior: STANDARD risk waves use @qa as gate agent; HIGH-RISK waves use @architect + @qa joint gate. The hardcoded value in execute-epic-plan.md (line 622, line 726) is the fallback when no gate_agent is specified in the execution plan wave definition.
